danger_signals.meaning: Aircraft shipment delays; operational disruption affecting player and competitors
Unpredictable negative impact on airline operations and passenger demand

danger_signals.visual_form: Financial status indicator transitioning to red/negative state
danger_signals.meaning: Bankruptcy threat; company faces reorganization or failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Aircraft procurement menu limited to faction-specific planes; negotiation difficulty indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Geographic/ideological constraints on operations; Moscow-based operations limited to Soviet aircraft

safety_signals.visual_form: Financial status indicator showing profit accumulation
safety_signals.meaning: Airline is operationally stable and growing

safety_signals.visual_form: World event notification with passenger volume increase indicators
safety_signals.meaning: Market opportunity; passenger demand surge in affected cities

safety_signals.visual_form: Route confirmation on world map; passenger flow indicators activating
safety_signals.meaning: Successful airport slot negotiation and route viability

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Aircraft procurement restricted to Soviet-manufactured planes only when headquartered in Moscow
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Moscow as headquarters triggers ideological faction affiliation; negotiation difficulty with Western nations increases; Western nation routes become restricted or costly

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Access and aircraft availability dependent on geographic headquarters choice; easier negotiations in Western cities (New York, London)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Western cities (New York, London, Tokyo) offer higher starting capital and aircraft inventory; cultural/political proximity affects negotiation success

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stock market interface displays buyable company shares; 51% ownership triggers assimilation visual/mechanical change
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stock ticker displays; company acquisition interface indicates faction shift when controlling majority shares

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Political restriction overlays on aircraft procurement menus; negotiation difficulty modifiers displayed; geographic faction affiliations visible on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI airlines behave according to geopolitical alignment; Soviet-headquartered airlines restricted to Soviet aircraft; Western-aligned airlines restricted from Eastern Bloc markets; passenger demand affected by political tensions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cold War restrictions visually removed from UI; aircraft procurement menu expanded globally; negotiation difficulty modifiers normalized
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All airlines gain access to global aircraft market; Eastern-Western borders open for competition; passenger demand and market dynamics normalize across ideological boundaries

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Available aircraft inventory limited to shorter-range propeller/early jet aircraft
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pacific Ocean crossing impossible initially; transcontinental routes unavailable; competitive pressure focused on regional dominance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aircraft procurement menu includes supersonic aircraft; longer-range jet inventory available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Intercontinental routes become viable and necessary; competition shifts to global dominance; longer routes become economically viable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Balance indicator turns negative; financial display highlights red; reorganization offer dialog appears after 4 consecutive negative quarters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Company operations continue but under reorganization constraint; player retains control but with reduced assets; AI competitors may exploit weakened position

environmental_storytelling.example: Choosing Moscow as headquarters immediately communicates Cold War alignment: Soviet aircraft only, Western negotiation penalties, restricted market access. Choosing New York signals Western alignment with abundant capital and aircraft access. Lima/Nairobi/Honolulu communicate developing-world market challenge with limited starting resources.

Olympic Games announced → passenger demand spikes to host city visually; Labor strike announced → aircraft delivery delays visible in procurement timeline; Perestroika initiated → visual policy restrictions lifted, market opens; natural disasters → affected regions show passenger/demand reduction

environmental_storytelling.technique: Aircraft Procurement Menu as Faction Reveal
environmental_storytelling.example: Soviet-headquartered airline sees only Cyrillic/Soviet aircraft names and models in procurement interface, making geopolitical affiliation explicit. Western airlines see Western aircraft manufacturers. Perestroika removes this visual faction separation.

environmental_storytelling.technique: City Network Growth as Visual Empire Building
environmental_storytelling.example: Win condition displays 22 cities required to be linked; map visually fills with routes; passenger volume accumulates on screen, communicated through quarterly reports showing visual/numerical growth toward 2.5-4.5 million passenger threshold

environmental_storytelling.technique: Quarterly Financial Reports as World State Indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Sales, expenses, profits, and passenger counts displayed after each quarter; world events that occurred that quarter shown immediately before results, teaching player cause-and-effect relationship between political/natural events and business outcomes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Restricted to Soviet aircraft procurement; negotiation success reduced with Western nations; focuses on Eastern Bloc city connectivity; avoids Western city routes unless economically forced

Procures Western aircraft; successful negotiation with Western nations; focuses on Western city dominance and Pacific/Atlantic intercontinental routes; avoids Eastern Bloc cities unless forced by market saturation

Suddenly gains access to full global aircraft market; begins expanding into previously restricted Eastern Bloc cities; negotiation difficulty globally normalized; market competition intensifies across all geographic zones

Company continues operations under reorganization; reduced operational capacity; reorganization dialog appears offering continued play under constraint; player forced into recovery strategy or loss condition
Displays in stock market interface; behaves as autonomous competitor; AI controls operations independently
Assimilates into player airline; operations merge with player airline; visual interface consolidates company into player corporation; no longer displays as independent entity

notes: Aerobiz communicates world-state and geopolitical situation primarily through mechanical and menu-based restrictions rather than explicit visual design. The Cold War is manifested as hard constraints in aircraft procurement and negotiation difficulty rather than visual theming. The game's manifestation layer relies heavily on: (1) procedural world events that interrupt gameplay and force player adaptation, (2) geographic headquarters selection as political affiliation signal, (3) financial UI state (red/negative balance) for danger signaling, and (4) aircraft menu availability as faction indicator. The Perestroika event serves as a dramatic world-state transition that removes mechanical restrictions entirely. Environmental storytelling occurs primarily through the juxtaposition of world events with quarterly financial outcomes, teaching the player to recognize cause-effect between geopolitical changes and business performance.
